title: telemetry-logs — compliance evidence
description: Logs carry more personal data than spans (message bodies, exception traces, HTTP payloads). Redaction is the primary compliance surface.

controls.evidence: Redactor pipeline strips PII BEFORE records leave the process. Rules: global key patterns (password, token, cookie, authorization), regex value patterns (credit card, SSN, IBAN), class-level #[RedactedForLogs] for domain models.

Log records may reference the erased subject via workspace_id + causer_id. GDPR Art. 17 does NOT require retroactive log erasure when logs are used for security / audit purposes. Sink retention naturally purges. Legal basis documented.
Third-party log sinks (Loki cloud, DataDog Logs, etc) are subprocessors. See subprocessors.json.
TLS transport to remote sinks. Sink credentials rotated quarterly.
Read access to logs gated by platform.telemetry.view permission at UI level. Sink-level access controlled by sink IAM (Grafana Cloud team perms, DataDog user roles).
logs are the primary source for incident forensics. Retention windows meet the 90-day minimum for security-event correlation.
Regex redactor patterns strip anything matching card-number regex before write. PAN / CVV / expiry never touched (they don't reach our code — payment provider tokenises client-side).
